AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN A HALF MILE NORTH OF FEDERAL. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED NEAR FEDERAL WHERE EIGHT STRUCTURES WERE IMPACTED ALONG MOUNTAIN SHADOW DRIVE AND MOUNTAIN SHADOW LANE. THE TORNADO MOVED SLOWLY TO THE SOUTH FOR A MILE BEFORE IT LIFTED AT 0332 PM. A MANUFACTURED HOME JUST EAST OF FEDERAL HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED, GARAGE DESTROYED, WHILE EXTERIOR WALLS REMAINED INTACT. THE TORNADO TOSSED NUMEROUS LIVESTOCK AND CAMPER TRAILERS. DEBRIS WAS STREWN APPROXIMATELY 500 YARDS.
